mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, with Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with mean forward returns below zero and t-statistic p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 300 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficients become statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit potential, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.
